hooks.name: Update README and Steering Before Push
hooks.description: Before a git push to a remote branch, updates README.md and .kiro/steering files to reflect the current project state.

action.prompt:
CONDITION — Only run this hook if the command being executed is a `git push` (i.e., the command string contains "git push"). If it is NOT a git push command, skip this hook entirely and proceed with the tool call unchanged.

If it IS a git push command, perform the following BEFORE allowing the push to proceed:

1. UPDATE README.md — Review the current README.md and update it to include:
   - Project summary: a brief one-paragraph overview of what the project is and what it does.
   - Project description: a more detailed explanation of the project's purpose, goals, and scope.
   Keep it concise and developer-friendly. Only update if the summary or description no longer accurately reflects the codebase.

2. UPDATE STEERING FILES — Create or update files in `.kiro/steering/` to capture project conventions and context:
   - `.kiro/steering/project-overview.md` — High-level description of the project architecture, tech stack, and key design decisions. Add front-matter with `inclusion: auto`.
   - `.kiro/steering/coding-standards.md` — Coding conventions, naming patterns, and style guidelines observed in the codebase. Add front-matter with `inclusion: auto`.

Only update sections that are affected by recent code changes. Do not rewrite unchanged sections. After updating the files, stage them and amend the last commit (or create a new commit with message "Docs: Update README and steering files") so they are included in the push.
